---
title: "Figure 4 diagnostic — R against IR at constant relative noise (r = 10), nsim 1000"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
A DIAGNOSTIC, NOT A PAPER FIGURE. It exists to answer one question before ~3300 CPU-h are spent:
does the instrumental noise cure R, or does the nsim-1000 data only LOOK cured because its intervals
are too wide to reject anything?

WHAT THIS DATA IS. figures/data/82b956f is not loose data: it is a complete, deliberate probe at
CONSTANT relative noise, noise label = 10 * N_ch, i.e. instrumental/gating ratio r = 10, for
macro_R / macro_MR / macro_NMR / macro_IR at N_ch 10 / 100 / 1000 / 10000. Its whole content is one
point per column of the staircase that figure_4.Rmd is built to show.

WHY IT CANNOT GO INTO figure_4. It is nsim 1000 while every other cell is nsim 10000, and
00_plan.md section 6 forbids pooling paper 1's band-A cells with anything at 1000. There is NOT ONE
cell present at both nsim levels, so the penalty cannot even be calibrated out. Hence a separate
file with the nsim in its name.

WHY LINES AND NOT A MAP. Each column carries exactly ONE noise level, so a filled contour is
impossible (it needs at least two distinct y values) and would in any case be the "provably blind"
two-level contour that figures_build_plan.md section 6 warns about. x = Delta*k_off, y = the value,
one line per algorithm.

WHY THE RAW MEAN AND ITS INTERVAL, NOT THE CI-SHRUNK VALUE. Everywhere else these quantities are
rendered CI-shrunk, so a cell whose interval covers the null collapses to exactly the null and draws
white. That is right for a map and WRONG here: it would convert "we cannot reject calibration" into
"calibrated", which is precisely the confound this file exists to expose. So the point estimate is
drawn with its 95% bootstrap band, and the reader sees whether the band contains the null because
the estimate sits there or because the band is wide.

BOTH ROWS ARE IN log10 UNITS with the null at 0: distortion is a ratio so log10(D) has its null at
log10(1) = 0, and the distortion-induced bias is already in log10 units with null 0. One null line,
one axis convention, four rows.

ANCHORS, same as the parent figure: distortion from battery_pool_G (theta_pool), bias from
battery_sim_G (theta_sim, because at the optimum the score vanishes and the bias is zero by
construction). Gaussian family throughout (_G files, Gaussian_* components).

NO ELLIPSE BLOCK. At nsim 1000, group_size 100 leaves 10 fits, and a variance from 10 fits carries
about 47% relative uncertainty; group_size 10 leaves 100 fits but at few channels its finite-sample
inflation reverses the sign of the N_ch direction. Neither is worth drawing.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r params}
ALGOS    <- c("macro_R", "macro_IR")
ALGO_LAB <- c(macro_R = "R", macro_IR = "IR")
ALGO_COL <- c(R = "#D55E00", IR = "#0072B2")

# the probe: noise label = 10 * N_ch in every column, so r = noise/N_ch = 10 throughout
GRID <- tibble::tribble(
  ~nch,  ~z,       ~nsim,
  10,    "100",    1000,
  100,   "1000",   1000,
  1000,  "10000",  1000,
  10000, "100000", 1000)
DIR <- "../data/82b956f"

PIDX  <- c(off = 1, Num_ch_mean = 5)
PLAB  <- c(off = "k[off]", Num_ch_mean = "N[ch]")
DIST  <- "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Gaussian_Information_Distortion"
BIAS  <- "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Induced_Bias"
```

```{r data}
findf <- function(nch, algo, z, nsim, kind) {
  f <- sprintf("%s/figure_3_G_nch_%d_nsim_%d_%s_noise_%s_%s.csv", DIR, nch, nsim, algo, z, kind)
  if (!file.exists(f)) stop("figure_4_diag: missing ", basename(f))
  f
}
cells <- expand_grid(algo = ALGOS, i = seq_len(nrow(GRID))) %>%
  mutate(nch = GRID$nch[i], z = GRID$z[i], nsim = GRID$nsim[i]) %>% select(algo, nch, z, nsim)
cat("figure_4_diag:", nrow(cells), "cells, all at r = noise/N_ch = 10, nsim 1000\n")

# one extractor, both anchors. Keeps the RAW mean and the two quantiles: no CI shrinking here.
grab <- function(kind, comp, diag_only) pmap_dfr(cells, function(algo, nch, z, nsim)
    read.csv(findf(nch, algo, z, nsim, kind), skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
      mutate(algo = algo, nch = nch, noise = z)) %>%
  filter(component_path == comp, param_index %in% PIDX,
         if (diag_only) param_index == param_col else TRUE,
         statistic == "value", probit %in% c("mean", "quantile")) %>%
  mutate(key = case_when(probit == "mean" ~ "m", quantile_level == 0.025 ~ "lo",
                         quantile_level == 0.975 ~ "hi", TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(key)) %>%
  group_by(algo, nch, noise, interval_in_tau, param_index, key) %>%
  summarise(v = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = key, values_from = v) %>%
  mutate(param = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)])

d <- bind_rows(
  grab("battery_pool_G", DIST, TRUE)  %>% mutate(what = "distortion",
                                                 across(c(m, lo, hi), log10)),
  grab("battery_sim_G",  BIAS, FALSE) %>% mutate(what = "bias")) %>%
  mutate(lx   = log10(interval_in_tau),
         alab = factor(ALGO_LAB[algo], levels = unname(ALGO_LAB)),
         prow = factor(sprintf("%s, %s", what, ifelse(param == "off", "k_off", "N_ch")),
                       levels = c("distortion, k_off", "distortion, N_ch",
                                  "bias, k_off", "bias, N_ch")),
         ncol_lab = factor(sprintf("atop(N[ch]==%d, sigma==%s)", nch, noise),
                           levels = sprintf("atop(N[ch]==%d, sigma==%s)", GRID$nch, GRID$z)))
stopifnot(nrow(d) > 0, !any(is.na(d$prow)), !any(is.na(d$ncol_lab)))
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 7.6}
X_BRK <- log10(c(.01, .1, 1))
g <- ggplot(d, aes(lx, m, colour = alab, fill = alab)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey30", linewidth = .35) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = .18, colour = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = .5) + geom_point(size = .7) +
  facet_grid(prow ~ ncol_lab, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = labeller(prow = label_value, ncol_lab = label_parsed)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = X_BRK, labels = c(".01", ".1", "1")) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = ALGO_COL) + scale_fill_manual(values = ALGO_COL) +
  labs(x = expression(Delta %.% k[off]), y = "log10 units (null = 0)", colour = NULL, fill = NULL,
       title = "R against IR at constant relative noise (noise = 10 x N_ch, r = 10), nsim 1000",
       subtitle = paste("point estimate with its 95% bootstrap interval, NOT CI-shrunk:",
                        "a band covering 0 may mean calibrated OR underpowered")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 8, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(0.14, "cm"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 8.5, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 7, colour = "grey30"),
        strip.text.y = element_text(size = 6.5, angle = 0), strip.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 6), legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.key.size = unit(.6, "lines"), legend.text = element_text(size = 7))
g
ggsave("Figure_4_diag_nsim1000.pdf", g, width = 7.0, height = 7.6)
```

```{r verdict}
# THE QUESTION THIS FILE EXISTS FOR, answered with numbers rather than by looking at the bands.
cat("\n=== 1. Is the point estimate at the null, or just consistent with it? ===\n")
cat("   |m| is the departure in log10 units; halfwidth is (hi-lo)/2. If |m| << halfwidth the cell is\n")
cat("   UNINFORMATIVE, not calibrated.\n\n")
print(as.data.frame(d %>% mutate(hw = (hi - lo) / 2) %>%
  group_by(prow, alab, nch) %>%
  summarise(med_absm = median(abs(m)), med_hw = median(hw),
            ratio = median(abs(m)) / median(hw), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(prow %in% c("distortion, k_off", "distortion, N_ch"))), digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n=== 2. THE POWER CHECK: how much wider are the nsim 1000 intervals? ===\n")
cat("   Same quantity, same algorithm, same N_ch, at nsim 10000 (noise 10, the highest absolute\n")
cat("   level that exists there). Widths are dominated by nsim, so this bounds the confound.\n\n")
DIRS10 <- c("../data/1c2ae6f", "../data/87889e6")
f10 <- function(nch, algo) { f <- file.path(DIRS10,
    sprintf("figure_3_G_nch_%d_nsim_10000_%s_noise_10_battery_pool_G.csv", nch, algo))
  h <- f[file.exists(f)]; if (length(h)) h[1] else NA_character_ }
w10 <- expand_grid(algo = ALGOS, nch = GRID$nch) %>%
  mutate(f = map2_chr(nch, algo, function(n, a) f10(n, a))) %>% filter(!is.na(f)) %>%
  pmap_dfr(function(algo, nch, f) read.csv(f, skip = 1) %>% mutate(algo = algo, nch = nch)) %>%
  filter(component_path == DIST, param_index == param_col, param_index %in% PIDX,
         statistic == "value", probit %in% c("mean", "quantile")) %>%
  mutate(key = case_when(probit == "mean" ~ "m", quantile_level == 0.025 ~ "lo",
                         quantile_level == 0.975 ~ "hi", TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(key)) %>%
  group_by(algo, nch, interval_in_tau, param_index, key) %>%
  summarise(v = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = key, values_from = v) %>%
  mutate(across(c(m, lo, hi), log10), hw = (hi - lo) / 2,
         alab = ALGO_LAB[algo], param = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)])

cmp <- d %>% filter(what == "distortion") %>% mutate(hw = (hi - lo) / 2) %>%
  group_by(alab, nch, param) %>% summarise(hw_1000 = median(hw), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(w10 %>% group_by(alab, nch, param) %>%
              summarise(hw_10000 = median(hw), .groups = "drop"),
            by = c("alab", "nch", "param")) %>%
  mutate(inflation = hw_1000 / hw_10000)
print(as.data.frame(cmp), digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n   sqrt(10) = 3.16 is the naive expectation if the interval scales as 1/sqrt(nsim).\n")

cat("\n=== 3. Verdict per cell: could a departure the size of R's at noise 0.1 have been seen? ===\n")
cat("   R's k_off distortion at noise 0.1 is about 1.44, i.e. log10 = 0.158. A cell whose halfwidth\n")
cat("   exceeds that could not have rejected calibration even if R were just as bad here.\n\n")
print(as.data.frame(d %>% filter(prow == "distortion, k_off") %>% mutate(hw = (hi - lo) / 2) %>%
  group_by(alab, nch) %>%
  summarise(med_hw = median(hw), can_detect_0.158 = median(hw) < 0.158, .groups = "drop")),
  digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)
```
